query:
INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES
-- Lesson 360031 - Introduction to Christian Apologetics
(360031, 'The word apologetics comes from the Greek word meaning what?', 'multiple_choice', '["To apologize","To give a defense","To argue","To preach"]', 'To give a defense', 1),
(360031, '1 Peter 3:15 instructs believers to be ready to give what?', 'multiple_choice', '["A sermon","A defense for the hope within","An argument","A testimony"]', 'A defense for the hope within', 2),
(360031, 'What is the primary purpose of apologetics?', 'multiple_choice', '["To win arguments","To defend and commend the faith","To prove skeptics wrong","To show intellectual superiority"]', 'To defend and commend the faith', 3),
(360031, 'Which approach to apologetics emphasizes evidence and reason?', 'multiple_choice', '["Presuppositional","Classical/Evidential","Fideistic","Experiential"]', 'Classical/Evidential', 4),
(360031, 'Presuppositional apologetics begins with what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Evidence","The authority of Scripture","Human reason","Experience"]', 'The authority of Scripture', 5),
(360031, 'According to 1 Peter 3:15, how should apologetics be done?', 'multiple_choice', '["With arrogance","With gentleness and respect","With aggression","With indifference"]', 'With gentleness and respect', 6),
(360031, 'Apologetics serves what role in evangelism?', 'multiple_choice', '["Replaces evangelism","Removes intellectual barriers","Is unnecessary","Proves everything"]', 'Removes intellectual barriers', 7),
(360031, 'Who is considered the father of Christian apologetics?', 'multiple_choice', '["Paul","Justin Martyr","Augustine","Aquinas"]', 'Justin Martyr', 8),
(360031, 'What is the relationship between faith and reason in apologetics?', 'multiple_choice', '["They are opposed","They complement each other","Faith replaces reason","Reason replaces faith"]', 'They complement each other', 9),
(360031, 'The goal of apologetics is ultimately what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Intellectual victory","Pointing people to Christ","Proving the Bible","Defeating atheism"]', 'Pointing people to Christ', 10),
(360031, 'Explain why both the heart and mind are important in Christian apologetics.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'Apologetics addresses intellectual barriers while evangelism addresses the heart. Both are needed for genuine conversion.', 11),

-- Lesson 360032 - The Existence of God - Classical Arguments
(360032, 'The cosmological argument argues from what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Design in nature","The existence of the universe","Moral law","Religious experience"]', 'The existence of the universe', 1),
(360032, 'Who formulated the Five Ways for proving God existence?', 'multiple_choice', '["Augustine","Thomas Aquinas","Anselm","Calvin"]', 'Thomas Aquinas', 2),
(360032, 'The teleological argument is also known as what?', 'multiple_choice', '["The moral argument","The design argument","The ontological argument","The cosmological argument"]', 'The design argument', 3),
(360032, 'The Kalam cosmological argument states that everything that begins to exist has what?', 'multiple_choice', '["A purpose","A cause","A design","An end"]', 'A cause', 4),
(360032, 'The fine-tuning argument points to what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Evolution","The precise constants of the universe","Human consciousness","Moral law"]', 'The precise constants of the universe', 5),
(360032, 'The moral argument for God existence is based on what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Human behavior","Objective moral values requiring a moral lawgiver","Cultural norms","Religious traditions"]', 'Objective moral values requiring a moral lawgiver', 6),
(360032, 'The ontological argument was first formulated by whom?', 'multiple_choice', '["Aquinas","Anselm","Augustine","Aristotle"]', 'Anselm', 7),
(360032, 'What does the argument from contingency demonstrate?', 'multiple_choice', '["Everything is necessary","A necessary being must exist","Nothing is contingent","The universe is eternal"]', 'A necessary being must exist', 8),
(360032, 'The argument from consciousness points to what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Brain chemistry","The immaterial nature of mind requiring an immaterial source","Evolution","Neuroscience"]', 'The immaterial nature of mind requiring an immaterial source', 9),
(360032, 'Classical arguments for God existence are best used how?', 'multiple_choice', '["As absolute proofs","As cumulative evidence pointing to God","To replace faith","To win debates"]', 'As cumulative evidence pointing to God', 10),
(360032, 'Summarize the cosmological argument and explain its significance for apologetics.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'Everything that begins has a cause; the universe began; therefore it has a cause - pointing to a transcendent Creator.', 11),

-- Lesson 360033 - The Problem of Evil and Suffering
(360033, 'The problem of evil is often stated as an argument against what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Religion","The existence of a good and powerful God","Christianity specifically","Morality"]', 'The existence of a good and powerful God', 1),
(360033, 'The logical problem of evil claims that God and evil are what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Compatible","Logically incompatible","Unrelated","Both necessary"]', 'Logically incompatible', 2),
(360033, 'The free will defense argues that evil exists because of what?', 'multiple_choice', '["God weakness","Human free choice","Natural processes","Divine punishment"]', 'Human free choice', 3),
(360033, 'Natural evil refers to what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Human sin","Suffering from natural events like earthquakes","Moral wrongdoing","Spiritual evil"]', 'Suffering from natural events like earthquakes', 4),
(360033, 'The evidential problem of evil argues that the amount of evil makes God existence what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Impossible","Improbable","Certain","Irrelevant"]', 'Improbable', 5),
(360033, 'A theodicy is an attempt to do what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Deny evil exists","Justify God in light of evil","Prove evil is good","Ignore suffering"]', 'Justify God in light of evil', 6),
(360033, 'The soul-making theodicy suggests suffering serves what purpose?', 'multiple_choice', '["Punishment","Character development","Entertainment","No purpose"]', 'Character development', 7),
(360033, 'How does the Christian worldview uniquely address suffering?', 'multiple_choice', '["By denying it","Through the suffering of Christ","By ignoring it","Through karma"]', 'Through the suffering of Christ', 8),
(360033, 'The greater good defense argues that God permits evil for what reason?', 'multiple_choice', '["He enjoys it","To bring about greater goods","He cannot stop it","No reason"]', 'To bring about greater goods', 9),
(360033, 'What is the Christian hope in the face of evil and suffering?', 'multiple_choice', '["Escape from the world","Ultimate redemption and restoration","Reincarnation","Annihilation"]', 'Ultimate redemption and restoration', 10),
(360033, 'Explain how the cross of Christ provides an answer to the problem of evil.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'God entered into human suffering through Christ, demonstrating His love and providing redemption and hope for ultimate restoration.', 11),

-- Lesson 360034 - The Reliability of the New Testament
(360034, 'How many manuscript copies of the New Testament exist?', 'multiple_choice', '["A few dozen","About 500","Over 5,800 Greek manuscripts","Only the originals"]', 'Over 5,800 Greek manuscripts', 1),
(360034, 'The time gap between the original NT writings and earliest copies is what?', 'multiple_choice', '["1000 years","500 years","Within decades to a century","Unknown"]', 'Within decades to a century', 2),
(360034, 'The bibliographical test examines what?', 'multiple_choice', '["The content of documents","The manuscript tradition and transmission","The authors beliefs","Modern interpretations"]', 'The manuscript tradition and transmission', 3),
(360034, 'The internal evidence test considers what?', 'multiple_choice', '["External sources","What the documents claim about themselves","Archaeological finds","Scientific accuracy"]', 'What the documents claim about themselves', 4),
(360034, 'The external evidence test looks at what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Internal consistency","Confirmation from outside sources","Manuscript copies","Author credentials"]', 'Confirmation from outside sources', 5),
(360034, 'Early church fathers quoted the NT extensively, demonstrating what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Their creativity","The early existence and acceptance of NT writings","Their memory","Nothing significant"]', 'The early existence and acceptance of NT writings', 6),
(360034, 'The criterion of embarrassment suggests what?', 'multiple_choice', '["The authors were embarrassed","Embarrassing details suggest authenticity","The NT is embarrassing","Authors lied"]', 'Embarrassing details suggest authenticity', 7),
(360034, 'Archaeological discoveries have done what to NT reliability?', 'multiple_choice', '["Disproved it","Consistently confirmed historical details","Been irrelevant","Raised more questions"]', 'Consistently confirmed historical details', 8),
(360034, 'The NT documents were written within what timeframe of the events?', 'multiple_choice', '["500 years","200 years","Within the lifetime of eyewitnesses","1000 years"]', 'Within the lifetime of eyewitnesses', 9),
(360034, 'Compared to other ancient documents, the NT manuscript evidence is what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Average","Far superior in quantity and quality","Inferior","Non-existent"]', 'Far superior in quantity and quality', 10),
(360034, 'Explain why the manuscript evidence for the New Testament is significant for its reliability.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'The abundance of early manuscripts allows scholars to reconstruct the original text with high confidence.', 11),

-- Lesson 360035 - The Resurrection of Jesus Christ
(360035, 'The resurrection of Jesus is central to Christianity because of what Paul said in which book?', 'multiple_choice', '["Romans","1 Corinthians 15","Galatians","Ephesians"]', '1 Corinthians 15', 1),
(360035, 'The empty tomb is significant because it rules out what theory?', 'multiple_choice', '["The swoon theory","The hallucination theory","The stolen body theory","The wrong tomb theory"]', 'The hallucination theory', 2),
(360035, 'The post-resurrection appearances were witnessed by how many people according to Paul?', 'multiple_choice', '["12","50","Over 500","A few"]', 'Over 500', 3),
(360035, 'The transformation of the disciples from fearful to bold suggests what?', 'multiple_choice', '["They were deceived","They encountered the risen Christ","They were lying","Nothing significant"]', 'They encountered the risen Christ', 4),
(360035, 'The conversion of Paul is significant because he was what before?', 'multiple_choice', '["A follower of Jesus","A persecutor of Christians","A Roman soldier","A Gentile"]', 'A persecutor of Christians', 5),
(360035, 'The conversion of James, Jesus brother, is significant because he was what before?', 'multiple_choice', '["A believer","A skeptic","A disciple","A Pharisee"]', 'A skeptic', 6),
(360035, 'The swoon theory fails because Roman crucifixion was what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Survivable","Designed to ensure death","Quick","Painless"]', 'Designed to ensure death', 7),
(360035, 'The hallucination theory fails to explain what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Individual experiences","Group appearances and the empty tomb","Psychological states","Grief"]', 'Group appearances and the empty tomb', 8),
(360035, 'The early creed in 1 Corinthians 15:3-7 dates to within how many years of the crucifixion?', 'multiple_choice', '["100 years","50 years","Within 5-7 years","200 years"]', 'Within 5-7 years', 9),
(360035, 'The resurrection is best explained by what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Natural causes","Jesus actually rose from the dead","Legend development","Mass hallucination"]', 'Jesus actually rose from the dead', 10),
(360035, 'Present the minimal facts argument for the resurrection of Jesus.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'Jesus died by crucifixion, disciples believed they saw Him risen, their lives were transformed, early proclamation, conversion of skeptics Paul and James.', 11);

(360034, 'The external evidence test looks at what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Internal consistency","Confirmation from outside sources","Manuscript copies","Author credentials"]', 'Confirmation from outside sources', 5),

-- Lesson 360035 - The Resurrection of Jesus Christ
(360035, 'The resurrection of Jesus is central to Christianity because of what Paul said in which book?', 'multiple_choice', '["Romans","1 Corinthians 15","Galatians","Ephesians"]', '1 Corinthians 15', 1),
